reviews.text: If Barry Benton works here, this place is awful

Listed on Super Lawyers as serving Hildago County. They do not. I was told it would be an undisclosed hourly rate and they would need a deposit. The attorneys were both in meetings and I was to get a call back the next day to discuss. No call back. If they don't care enough to call back, they likely put the same care into their cases.

If I could leave zero stars, I would. The worst experience I have ever had. My appt with Mr Orozco was on May 5th at 4 pm. I do not live in Los Fresnos, so it was a phone meeting that he was late for. Mr Orozco assured me he would call back by Wednesday, May 7th, after he consulted with another associate. After repeated calls from me to his office with no response from him or his assistant, I finally received a call on May 21, only to be told they could not take my case but would gladly refer me to another practice. My case involves guardianship for my elderly mom. It may not be a ‘big money’ case but it is very important and emotional to me. Apparently, Mr Orozco felt it wasn’t a priority to have the decency to call when he said he would, return my calls, or even ask his assistant to return my calls in a timely manner. Oh did I forget to mention, his firm didn’t hesitate to take $200 before he would even meet with me on May 5th. Others have left good reviews but PLEASE read my review and take my feedback to heart. This is the only review I have ever written because I know life gets in the way and things happen. I felt in my heart it was warranted to share in hopes Mr Orozco and the practice can improve their performance in dealing with any clients, potential or not.

I can’t say enough good things about this law firm. From the moment I first reached out, I was met with such kindness, professionalism, and genuine care. It’s clear that this team not only knows the law inside and out but also truly cares about the people they’re helping.

They made a stressful situation feel manageable and even gave me peace of mind during moments I felt overwhelmed. Every question I had was answered with patience and clarity. They never made me feel like just another case — they treated me like a real person, with compassion and respect.

What really sets them apart is how lovely they all are to work with. They’re warm, honest, and incredibly supportive, and I always felt like they had my back every step of the way. It’s rare to find that combination of top-notch legal skill and sincere human kindness — but this firm has both.

If you’re looking for a law firm that truly cares and delivers excellent results, look no further. I’m beyond grateful for everything they’ve done for me.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Estefania,
At VAMOS Law Group, we take all feedback seriously and are committed to serving our clients with respect, transparency, and care.

We do want to clarify that the decision in your case was based on underlying factors outside of our control, not the work or effort provided by your case manager. Throughout your case, our team went above and beyond to support you — we filed a motion to appeal on your behalf completely free of charge, as well as, provided you with a letter of representation to assist with your matter.

Additionally, any time you called with questions or concerns, you were always assisted by your case manager, the office administrator, or the attorney, ensuring that your needs were addressed promptly.

Following the decision on your case, you also spoke directly with the attorney, who advised you on the next legal steps that would best suit your situation.

At VAMOS Law Group, our priority has always been — and continues to be — supporting our clients and our community with honesty, compassion, and integrity.

— Valdez, Monarrez, & San Miguel

reviews.text: Very bad service, they moved to a different city without informing me since we had a case with them, they never answer the phone and when they do it's Conchis, the secretary who never has an answer because all she can say is... I don't know, I'm just the secretary, when we went to immigration we realized that the vaccines were expired which they never informed us, all of this delayed the process and they didn't even contact me after a month, this is ridiculous, not at all professional and too expensive. Please I recommend that you don't risk using their services, the truth is they always say that one should look for a professional but this one was very unprofessional.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Evangelina,
Thank you for sharing your comment. We value your opinion and would like to address the concerns you have raised. Due to the confidentiality of your case, we cannot discuss the specific details of the process, but we are happy to provide clarification on the points you have raised.

We understand that the closure of our Houston office was an unexpected inconvenience and we regret that it affected your experience. Please note that a notice regarding the office changes was sent directly to your last registered home address, and at that time, you were given the option to discontinue our services if you were not satisfied with the transfer of your file.

We also acknowledge your concerns about Conchis, our receptionist. As a receptionist, she is not authorized to offer legal advice, nor is she involved in the process of your case. However, every time you contacted us with questions or requests for updates regarding your husband's case, we made sure to schedule an appointment with an attorney to address your needs.

Regarding your husband's USCIS interview, we offered to send him to a local attorney in Houston at no additional cost, but you declined. As you know, we are unable to open the medical examination documents, as they are sent sealed to immigration. Unfortunately, this meant we were unaware that the medical examinations had expired at the time of the interview. Once we received the application in the mail, we contacted you immediately. Please understand that we have no control over the time it takes USCIS to mail the documents.

We regret that you were not completely satisfied with our services, but we are pleased to hear that we were able to obtain approval in your husband's case. Congratulations on this important achievement!

Thank you again for your comment.

reviews.text: They are the worst, I swear. I went very respectfully to the secretary, I told her about my brother's case, and for a whole week she humiliated me for not being rich. And she even dared to lie. It all started on Monday. I went, she told me she would call me on Tuesday. She called me, told me the prices, and said I could come for my free consultation on Wednesday around 10 or 11 am. I asked for permission to be late at work. I called on Wednesday and they said the lawyer was busy and that I could come tomorrow, Thursday, at 10 am. Okay, I was very patient. On Thursday I called and she told me she was also very busy, but that they would call me back at 2 pm and that my consultation would be by cell phone. Nothing! They didn't call. Today, Wednesday, almost two weeks later, I called the secretary and asked her why they humiliate us just for not having money. The shameless woman tells me that the lawyer called my cell phone—pure lies! I checked my phone, there's no call from that day, and she still blames me and tells me not to call again. Oh my God! This is so you realize that lawyers and secretaries humiliate you horribly just for not being rich or millionaires. But I guarantee you one thing: those of us who have very little have more than those who have money.

reviews.text: Thank you I have received my check

As of January 2022, my mother has been waiting over two years to be named executor of her brother's estate.  It has been over 6 months since my mother has turned in all requested documents. In fact the only reason she turned in something 6 months ago is because I got involved. We've received no timeline in regard to the resolution of this probate case.

Update - February 29, 2024 - 4 years and counting to have a one asset estate resolved.  No timelines - No estimate for resolution of my uncles estate.  Absolutely avoid this attorney for all estate matters.

In response to the Owner:

The original consult was so bad my mother was convinced she had to pay the mortgage and upkeep for her dead brothers home for 4 years.  Only to recoup less than what she put into the whole ordeal.   Here is what should have been explained to her clearly upfront "You don't need the services of an attorney.  There is no will, you will be better off not doing anything.  Do not pay a single penny to anyone." -  But you did not.  She was poorly informed and payed you a fee and began stressful 4 year ordeal of payments and court appointments. Conclusion,  talk to a good attorney, not Jason Mann, save your self time and money.
reviews.ownerResponse: This is a dependent administration. There was no will nor executor. With over 30 beneficiaries to find and serve during COVID. And a house to be sold needed court permission to be listed and sold. The duration was not atypical. Have a will and save your family the headache of dependent administration folks!!

Do not hire this attorney if your family member is detained. They promote a lot of uscis and passport cases, which is good....I'm sure they are excellent, but do not hire them if your family member is detained by ICE. The reason I say this is because a good attorney will go consult with the detained person (at the detention center) to see what is the BEST form of relief for them and push for that. An attorney is not supposed to go based on the intake form he conducted with the client 4 years ago. It blows my mind how an attorney can not even talk to the client about what she wants to fight or her options when detained, just talk to the family members and let them decide.

reviews.text:
I believe God placed attorney Jaime Diez and his assistant, Ms. Heidi Rosebaum, in our path. They were always there for us and were very attentive to our case.

First, they helped me obtain my American citizenship in a very short time.

Then, once I became a citizen, I applied for permanent residency for my wife, which was approved no more than six months after requesting the change of status.

We are very happy, and I sincerely recommend the entire team at Diez & Crane, who patiently and professionally explained the entire process to us from the beginning.

Today, all I have left to say is: THANK YOU to attorney Jaime Diez and his entire team!

See you in three years when my wife applies for her American citizenship.

Thank you so much, Ms. Heidi, for everything!
Good service

Damaris's service is terrible. She should be fired. Thanks to her, I haven't been able to get my immigration paperwork sorted to this day. When I went to see if they could help me with my documents, my husband is a citizen, and she told me I could get it sorted through my previous husband since I suffered domestic violence. I never wanted to get my paperwork sorted for that reason. When I went to her office, I was already divorced, and my husband, for whom I needed to get my paperwork sorted, didn't want her to submit it through my ex-husband because she said it was easier. My husband told her he didn't want her to do that, but she said it was better that way, and she submitted it anyway. We asked her three times that we didn't want to do it that way, and she said it was better. A year and four months later, they sent a denial letter because I was married to someone else there. Thanks to her, I still haven't been able to get my paperwork sorted. She's a terrible woman and completely useless.
reviews.ownerResponse: I'm very sorry about this, you can schedule an appointment with Attorney Diez.

I would recommend John Shergold to anyone who needs a lawyer. He has taken care of my case that is coming to a year in October. He is straight forward and to the point. He is short and sweet. I never had to worry about work being done because he kept tabs on my case and informed me of where he is at in the process. Still a ways to go with my case but I would not be able to say my charges have been dismissed with out John and his firm and the grace of God. Again, if your looking for someone to get to the point and get things done, choose Hodge and Shergold!

Called to schedule a consultation. Shergold called me back spent 5 minutes asking me how I got his number (like who referred me) then asked me questions to which I could not answer. I began reading off a document (the whole reason for the consult) to which he cut me off saying he didn’t need to me read the document, in fact he didn’t need to “put his eye balls on it”. He said he could take me in and it was going to be $200 for a 1.5 hr consult. He said we’d likely have to go to court so I’ll need an atty, mind you my request was to simply translate a document into terms I could understand. I told him that I would think about going in the following day, and before I could finish my sentence, he cut me off again and said “well good luck then. I’m not going to waste my time over the phone with you. I can’t just give away my services I need to make money. I’m highly educated. You going to need me but good luck. You know where to find me.” His response was unwarranted; I never asked for a handout! I was willing to pay and he’d know that if he hadn’t jumped to conclusions. He ended our conversation by hanging up on me. And for this, he gets ZERO stars, if I could. Attorneys should have empathy and compassion for their clients. This money driven behavior also drives clients away.

FYI, I uploaded my document to chat GBT which successfully translated the document from “legalese” into laymen’s terms. I was also able to negotiate my package to increase my payout and insurance coverage exponentially (thousands of dollars) without the aid of THIS attorney.

reviews.text:
My brother and I were Nicole's clients.
My brother was in detention.
Nicole was very kind and compassionate, answering our calls and messages, consulting with us, and advising us on the best course of action in various situations.

Nicole did everything within her power.
Even when situations were difficult, she remained calm and understood that, as my brother's relatives, we were nervous.
She was understanding.

Nicole did her job, for which we are grateful.
Nicole was unable to continue our case.
We decided to continue with a lawyer who would not have a language barrier.
And we won the case.
However, it was Nicole who started it all.
For which we say THANK YOU!

reviews.text:
I never thought a simple drive home would change everything, but one rainy afternoon on a busy Texas highway, a distracted driver slammed into my car from behind. The impact spun me around, and suddenly I was dealing with whiplash, back pain that wouldn't quit, mounting medical bills, and an insurance company that seemed more interested in lowball offers than fair treatment. I felt lost and overwhelmed until I found Lopez Law Group.

From the very first free consultation, Attorney Fernando J. Lopez and his team made me feel heard and supported. They explained the entire personal injury process in plain language no confusing legal jargon just clear steps on what to expect. They handled every call with the insurance adjusters, gathered police reports, medical records, and even expert opinions to build a strong case. Their knowledge of car accident claims, truck accidents and workplace injuries really showed through.

What stood out most was how dedicated they were. They fought hard for every dollar I deserved, pushing back against the insurance tactics that try to minimize payouts. In the end, they secured a settlement that covered my medical expenses, lost wages, pain and suffering, and more far better than anything I could have negotiated alone. Throughout, communication stayed open with quick responses to my questions, and updates kept me in the loop without me having to chase them down.
If you're searching for the best car accident attorney in Texas, a top personal injury lawyer who truly cares, an experienced accident lawyer for car crashes, truck wrecks, or workplace injuries, or just a reliable injury attorney who works on a no win no fee basis with free case evaluations—look no further than Lopez Law Group.

They're bilingual (English and Spanish) and  available 24/7. I highly recommend them to anyone in the Rio Grande Valley (McAllen, Harlingen, Edinburg, Weslaco, Brownsville), Houston, Dallas, Austin, or anywhere in Texas dealing with a serious accident. They turned a stressful nightmare into real justice and peace of mind.

Thank you, Lopez Law Group—you're the real deal!

Luis Hernández —— If you need an attorney DO NOT hire this man. He’s been sitting on my probate case for almost 2 years and has done absolutely nothing. He withdrew from representing me 3 days before we had court, because “the judge was going to be pissed” because he’s been dragging my case and a witness report was incomplete. A report that was incomplete for months but he decides to take note days before court. He filed 2 or 3 times for my case since March 2024, and one of the times I was the one filling out the information for him.

Initially he asked me how much I had paid when I asked for my money back and now he’s ignoring my messages. I’m out thousands of dollars and it’s not fair. He did NOTHING. And I’m not the only one he’s done this to.

I’ve had to hire another attorney. I absolutely don’t mind paying for services rendered but like I said, he did NOTHING but waste my time and my kids time.

reviews.text: Horrible experience! A year ago, they eagerly took my case and had all the pertinent information they needed. I was assured they could and would handle my case.  Every month or so, the attorney and staff I was assigned to, were changed without notification to me. This happened 5 times. The left hand never knew what the right hand was doing. I got so much run around and excuses, it was pathetic. I was never offered any assistance with medical or anything else for that matter. While suffering with a major injury, I was ignored and treated like I was of no importance whatsoever. My fifth and final assigned attorney, after 2 months of supposed service, informed they would not be perusing my case, though they had no different information than what I gave them to begin with. I now have to start the whole agonizing process of finding a decent and trustful attorney. Their motto, "We Put You First" is a joke. I never felt first after the initial day they accepted my case. By the way, the attorneys were in Corpus Christy though I live in the RGV. I should have been assigned ones in Brownsville, where I could have at least seen them face to face and maybe not have been treated so negatively.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Ms. Williams,
Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. We are truly sorry to hear about your frustrations and the challenges you faced while dealing with your injury. After carefully reviewing the photos, reports, and all details of your slip-and-fall incident, our team was unable to identify a defect or unsafe condition on the stairs that would allow us to establish liability against the property owner. Because of this, we ultimately had to withdraw so that you would still have the opportunity to seek another firm that might view the case differently or have a different approach.

Regarding communication and attorney assignments, we recognize that this was frustrating for you and we apologize for any confusion caused by those transitions. We continually work to improve our internal processes to ensure a smoother experience for clients.

Our Brownsville and RGV teams frequently assist clients throughout the Valley, but we understand your concern about distance and face-to-face communication. All you had to do was request a lawyer at our Brownsville office and we could have made this adjustment.

We sincerely wish you the best in finding a firm that is able to assist you, and we hope you find relief and recovery from your injuries soon. If you need copies of any documents or have questions about your file, please feel free to contact us.

Erin was appointed by the judge to ensure the best interests of my daughter (who was four years old at the time), since both her father and I were represented by attorneys. Prior to court, she confronted me and accused me of coaching my daughter to lie about the traumatic events she experienced. She further stated that the CPS caseworker would support her and confirm these claims. This was despite the fact that two doctors, two nurses, and a therapist had all confirmed my daughter’s account.
When a child is taken to the hospital for an examination, they are separated from the parent and questioned to determine whether the story changes under different circumstances, which helps identify if the child is being coerced. A full medical examination is also performed. In my daughter’s case, both doctors confirmed that the abuse occurred. For Erin to accuse me of fabricating my daughter’s testimony was devastating. At no point did my daughter ever change her story.
When I first met Erin, her initial comment was, “Well, this is a very detailed story for a four-year-old.” I dismissed the remark and gave her the benefit of the doubt, believing she would protect my daughter’s best interests. The only reason she did not present her accusations to the judge was because the CPS caseworker, who was allegedly going to support her claims, failed to appear at the scheduled Zoom hearing. I later reported that CPS caseworker and received a written response confirming that my concerns were valid and that the case had not been thoroughly investigated. Additionally, my attorney contacted my child’s father’s attorney, who admitted he had never spoken with the CPS caseworker and that Erin was the one who relayed the false claim that I had been lying.
Erin’s conduct was deeply unprofessional and harmful, and I strongly caution others against seeking her assistance. She is now running for judge, but someone who has demonstrated a willingness to misrepresent the truth should not be entrusted with such a position of authority.

One of the worst attorneys. Extremely unprofessional, disrespectful, and did absolutely nothing to help. Please don’t waste your time or money with this person. It’s heartbreaking that people like her exist. She’s an absolute joke.

Many times, he calmed down my stress and fear about my child’s situation.  As a mother, I would never have expected to be hiring an attorney for my child, but situations nowadays escalate very easily.  Don’t let external voices tell you “Everything will be fine” or “It is just a simple question and answer meeting”, if you are questioning yourself about getting an attorney, get yourself one, call Mauricio!  We are very pleased with the outcome of the case; Mauricio was able to represent my child very professionally and mediated in front of every single law representative throughout the case and was able to get my child’s case dismissed.  Also, when it comes to finances, Mauricio has payment plans that can help you afford the help you need, don’t let that stop you making contact.   Y cuando dice que hablan Español, es verdad!!!  Si eres como yo y se te complica el Inglés cuando estás bajo estres, Mauricio te traduce a un perfecto español todo el proceso para que te sea más fácil entender la situación por la que tú o tu familiar esten pasando.

reviews.text:
A family member was on probation  in Cameron county for a DWI.  Unfortunately, he picked up a PI in another county which resulted in an MTR being filed. I went on google and came across Mauricio. I decided to give him a call since there were so many positive reviews about his communication. They were definitely right. He took the time to talk to us and was always a text message away.

We were eager to get this case settled quickly to avoid getting arrested and transferred down to Cameron county ( which would have taken weeks). Within a week of the warrant going out, we were able to get the case resolved and the MTR being withdrawn. He set up the court date for us so my family member could turn himself in. We were able to walk out the of the court house within an hour, warrant withdrawn and not a single day spent in jail.

We are so thankful to Mauricio for resolving this case quickly and with no jail time.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience! We're happy to hear that Mr. Garcia's representation resulted in a positive outcome for your family.

As an American living in the UK over the past 10 years, returning back to the US, with a British wife. My understanding of the US Immigration laws were extremely vague. We sought and spoke to several immigration attorney's. We eventually ended up speaking with Attorney Gabriela Lozano from Pacheco Couceiro. She was through and spoke to us using  layman explanations and the rights we had. She was so reassuring. And more importantly, she made the entire process easy and stress free. Gabriela consistently reached out to us throughout the entire process to keep us informed of what was being processed, until the day we got the approval.

Thank you Gabby and the entire team at Pacheco Couceiro.

These lawyers are useless, please don't be fooled. They make you sign a contract in which they are supposedly serious. They ask you for money first... they don't fight your case and then they want to get more money from you and do nothing. They disappear... be careful, it happened to me today, January 28, 2025... I asked them to make a document... so I could have another hearing and they wanted more money. And they make the excuse that nothing can be done and they shamelessly steal the money...

I do not recommend them, they take your money and then disappear. At first my experience with them was very good, Attorney Pachecho took my husband's case the day before his hearing and did what he could. The attorney always responded to my texts and calls. My husband tested negative before a racist judge, and the office recommended writing a letter. Then my husband tried to contact the attorney several times regarding an issue where he was detained. I contacted the assistant Roxana several times to schedule an appointment so my husband could speak freely with the attorney about a situation, but he never did, and the attorney never showed up or called him. His response was very short, basically saying they washed their hands of my husband's case because he received a negative result and the asylum department was not going to reconsider his case. The problem was that the issue my husband wanted to discuss with the attorney was regarding negligence and abuse and some documents that he wanted to clarify before signing them. But no, Roxana lied, she said she scheduled the appointment twice and never did, my husband was left waiting 3 months for the appointment or conference and he never did and nothing happened until he was deported.

If you seek their help in a deportation case, it's so they act promptly. All they care about is getting paid by the family. The reason he didn't have a serious crime was illegal border entry.
In the end, they didn't stop the order because they were incompetent and insufficient. If they know they're not capable, why commit to it? We had an extremely disappointing experience with Nat Perez and Noah Perez. Throughout the case, it felt like they put forth very little effort and showed almost no willingness to consider the client’s perspective or suggestions. The refused to entertain and other reasoning even when those suggestions were specific, logical, and legally grounded.

They repeatedly dismissed Isaac’s account of events, refused to meaningfully listen, and ignored crucial insights he tried to provide about the case. Several motions were filed, but every one of them was denied, and there was no real sense of strategy, follow-through, or urgency behind their efforts.
Their communication with the family was cold, dismissive, and at times deceptive. They gave the impression that they knew best, but their results and behavior told a different story. Isaac had multiple witnesses willing to speak, but after meeting with these attorneys, none of them felt comfortable testifying. These attorneys discouraged them, and this was spoken from the mouths of the witnesses.

Even at sentencing, they rejected his character witness without any clear reason, shutting down a critical opportunity for Isaac to present support on the record.

In our experience, these attorneys were stubborn, inflexible, and unwilling to collaborate with the very person whose life was on the line. I would not recommend them to anyone seeking advocates who truly listen, communicate honestly, and fight for their client regardless of guilt or innocence.

I called to get more information about services regarding my situation and potential expenses. I’m not sure if it was due to the complexity of my case (which is why I’m seeking legal help in the first place), but the attorney I spoke with (Nate Perez) was very rude and dismissive. That interaction alone made it clear that I would not want someone with that attitude representing me in any capacity. First impressions matter, and unfortunately, this one was very unprofessional.
10/10 experience you need an attorney this is your guy helped me with my case and got it dismissed quick.I recommend him to anybody that needs an attorney does miracle work.

I don’t even know if I have the words to fully express how grateful my husband and I are to Nat and Noah Perez. They walked our family through one of the scariest, most overwhelming times of our lives. As someone who had never been anywhere near a courtroom before, stepping into a criminal trial felt like stepping into another world for us. But Nat and Noah were with us through every step, calming our fears, answering any questions, and never once making us feel like “just another case.”

They treated my husband with dignity and our family with so much kindness. Nat and Noah were so prepared, so confident, and so clearly dedicated to fighting for him against what has now been proven to be bogus allegations. By the Grace of God and their hard work, my husband was found NOT GUILTY of all charges.

Now, for the first time in so long, we can finally breathe again and start putting our lives back together. There are no words big enough to thank Nat and Noah for giving our family this chance to heal. If you are ever in need of a defense team, I pray you find your way to them. They are truly a blessing and exceptional at what they do.

reviews.text: When the letter arrived stating that my daughter no longer was considered disabled,  I panicked. She is living with me so I know the disability is real. I called 211 to see what this meant and what I had to do, and after waiting an hour on the phone, I finally spoke to someone. The person suggested a legal representative and gave me a few numbers. Some didn’t answer, others were disconnected. I’m so glad that “RGV Disability” answered right away.  The young lady that answered knew just what I was going through, after a few questions, she set up an appointment to speak to the attorney, Mr. Raines.  When he called and explained the process, what to expect and what he was going to do, it was such a relief to me and my daughter! He was successful and he won the case. No one should have to go through this alone, not when we can call Mr. Justin Raines at RGV Disability! Best choice I ever made. My daughter and I both highly recommend him to anyone going thru a similar situation. You are not alone, we have Justin Raines to fight the legal battles for us. Thank you RGV Disability for being there at our time of need!🙏

reviews.text: I do not recommend Gustavo Elizondo as an attorney. I retained him to represent my son and feel he didnt do a good job. Don't waste your money, a public defender will put more effort into your case than he does.  I thought about changing attorney mid-way through but he said I would still have to pay him the full about because he charges a set fee regardless of the work he puts in or the outcome of the case. He did get a bond reduction but apparently that's all he's good at. It was hard getting a hold of him as I had to call the automated system, talk to his secretary, and schedule a phone call before I could talk to him every time. Then after waiting days to talk to him, he would try to rush me off the phone. Any updates or information he would give me, I already had from looking at the District Court website or getting the police report myself. I put more work in then he did. He tried to convince my son to take the first deal the prosecutor offered so he didn't have to put any actual work in. Thank God I stayed on him and didn't give up. My son ended up getting a decent deal but I feel a public defender coulda got him the same deal for free.

Outstanding Lawyer!!!!

My husband and I were going through an absolute nightmare dealing with our home insurance claim. After months of trying to resolve things on our own, we felt frustrated and defeated—until we hired Trey Mendez. From the very beginning, he treated us with professionalism, respect, and genuine care. We were never just another case to him; he saw us as real people going through a tough time.

Trey maintained constant communication, ensuring we always knew what was happening and checking in on our well-being. His expertise and dedication quickly turned our situation around, resolving our case much faster than we ever expected. Thanks to him, we regained the confidence and peace of mind that we had lost in the process.

We can’t thank Trey enough for his hard work and the way he truly fought for us. If you need a lawyer who is not only skilled but also compassionate and committed, Trey Mendez is the one you want by your side.

Highly recommend!

I highly recommend Trey for all your insurance claim needs.

reviews.text:
Trey is truly an exceptional attorney! After our accident, we felt completely hopeless. The driver at fault lied about their insurance, providing us with a policy that wasn’t even valid at the time of the accident. To make matters worse, their insurance denied our claim, leaving us with no answers and no justice.

But Trey went above and beyond to turn things around for us! He immediately conducted a thorough investigation, uncovering that the driver got the insurance after the accident and that the insurance agency was actually owned by the family of the driver at fault. Trey’s dedication and attention to detail were incredible.

Trey built a strong case and filed a lawsuit. He not only held the driver accountable but also secured compensation for the damages to our vehicle and medical bills.

If it weren’t for Trey’s persistence, we might have given up when the insurance denial came through. But because of him, we found hope and justice when we thought it was out of reach!

Thank you, Trey, for everything you’ve done for us. You are truly a lifesaver!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much Alexa.  Your review means a lot to me and I was happy to be able to work this case to a resolution for you.  At the Mendez Law Firm we take all of our personal injury and insurance claim cases seriously and give them the utmost attention to detail.

My experience was terrible. I contacted the office for help with a case, and the lawyer immediately misled me, saying my relative was entitled to bail. As a lawyer, he should know that an arrived alien is not entitled to bail because the judge lacks jurisdiction in their case. Okay, I called the office, and they told me I had to pay to review any documents. Okay, I paid. It's very important to clarify (they require an affidavit of support for bail), and I couldn't obtain it. When I spoke with them to see if there was any possibility of a partial refund, they refused. So, they charged me $1,500 without doing any work. To me, that's a scam.

They loooveee to interrupt you constantly and do not let you speak or get a single word out. They claim to record their phone calls but get mad when you do the same. When you play them the phone call recorded  where they asked for a record instead of police reports they don’t even apologize for using the wrong term instead they tell you “you should have known what I meant”. They tell you they will ask for a bond hearing for your family member detained but then will go see said family member and tell them to auto deport themselves. Then say they did their job. Then once you send that 6th doc they call you in 48 hrs only to say you’re now missing more documents. Then blame you for not sending everything the 1st time. DO NOT PAY THEM!!! HORRIBLE PEOPLE!! The reason the lawyer gets mad at you when you tell him his assistants are rude is because he is rude too. JOSE RODRIGUEZ is okay with his assistants being RUDE AND DISRESPECTFUL to his clients but gets mad when you try to defend yourself. reviews.text: My husband was detained in Port Isabel he met Tostado there.  Tostado offered her services and guaranteed she could bail him out. They stated that the $500 was going to be reimbursed to us it’s been more than 6weeks of that and haven’t received anything. I’ve been calling the office but they state that the attorney has to sign the check and hasn’t done it.
Paola Tostado is a very good lawyer ❤️

I've never left a bad review on Google, but after a long time, I'm finally doing so just to tell you not to hire Paola. Don't waste your money. Many people recommend her blindly; at first, you think she'll be your savior, but no, she's the complete opposite. She just takes your money, and once she has it, there's no benefit for you. Personally, I paid her to represent me in my bail case, and on the day of my court hearing, she didn't even show up via Zoom or represent me. I wish her all the best, but she's irresponsible.

The first communication I had with this law firm was in the early part of June 2025 . My nephew had been detained by ICE and sent to the detention center in Brownsville Texas. I filled out some forms with personal sensitive information ( they asked me to block them out..which I did) The whole process from their intervention with ICE to my nephew’s release took a little less than 4 months. My experience with this law firm is great and I will recommend them to anyone who needs help with immigration cases.

Tommy B Martins (Tucson,Arizona)

For the last 7 years of my marriage, I have stood beside, advocated for, and fought with my Husband to pull his son from a previous relationship out of a long-standing dangerous situation. We have paid for attorneys in three states, some states paying multiple attorneys, to track down and secure safety and accountability for my step-son. The pain associated with a child who is weaponized against one parent’s side of the family is devastating, and it wreaked havoc not only on us as parents but on our children and extended family members as well.
We met Liz after being referred by CPS. Immediately, she set to work finding the fastest way of securing temporary sole custody for my Husband while we prepared for the long haul in obtaining a final order. She took our safety concerns and the traumatic history of our son to heart, and was successful in placing safeguards with his bio-mom while giving us the ability to keep him in a stable family unit that was in the middle of a move with the military. It has taken us exactly one year to get a final order that was achieved by successful mediation and Liz was able to tailor it to our needs. It wasn’t easy, nothing that involves someone who struggles with substance abuse ever is, but once she was ultimately able to lay out what would potentially happen in a trial, Liz got her point across and was successful in getting an agreement signed.
From the bottom of my heart, I highly recommend Liz for all of your family law needs. She will give you reality and address your fears/anxieties. She will listen to your concerns and create a plan that fits your family. She will fight for you and your family, and given the chance she will be successful.

Thank you, Liz for giving us the ability to raise our son in a safe and loving environment.

I have been in a contentious custody battle for years and have used several attorneys. Ms. Valdez Garza took my case on in 2018 and has lived up to the “La Leona” moniker. I am a father to a 13 year old son and am active duty military. I reside out of the state but communication and information flow between Ms. Valdez Garza and myself has always been great. She is passionate about protecting the safety of my child and securing the rights that I deserve.

The other party involved has a history of substance abuse and Ms. Valdez Garza fought hard to ensure that the safeguards were put in place in order to help secure the safety of my son when visiting his Mother and family. She went above and beyond to try and contact the other party and ultimately got someone who was adamant about not wanting to agree to anything to successfully mediate before trial.

My family and I are grateful for the hard work and representation that Ms. Valdez Garza gave on my behalf. Thanks to her my son is in a safe place now that I have custody of him. I would recommend the services of Ms. Valdez Garza to anyone who needs an attorney who will fight for their rights and will do all that she can to make sure you receive the representation you deserve. Thank you, Liz!

-Stephen Clark

Ried Pecina Trial Lawyers are the best truck accident lawyers to go to in Brownsville, Tx. I couldn’t be more satisfied with the outcome. From the beginning they cared about my recovery and my rights.

What really stood out was their deep understanding of state and federal trucking regulations. They knew exactly what to look for—driver logs, maintenance records, and black box data—and used that information to hold the trucking company accountable. Their legal strategy was smart, aggressive, and highly effective.

Throughout the process, they made sure I never felt alone. Thanks to their hard work, I received a large settlement that covered my medical bills, lost wages, and compensation for the pain and disruption the accident caused.

If you’re dealing with the aftermath of a truck accident, I highly recommend Ried Pecina Trial Lawyers. They are highly skilled at what they do, they care about their clients and they get big results.
